# Agility Dogs
### Westminster Agility Masters: Handler’s Edge

## Core Vision
A premium dog agility sports-simulation where the player controls the **handler** in third-person, while the **dog is an intelligent athletic partner** driven by systemic AI.

The game should deliver:
- authentic handler-first gameplay
- believable dog intelligence and momentum
- real agility adjudication and faulting
- broadcast-quality presentation
- replay spectacle
- reactive crowd energy
- commentary and arena storytelling
- a staged production path from proof of concept to premium 1.0

This is **not** an arcade pet game and **not** a novelty dog runner.  
It is a **premium sports-sim** with a presentation quality target comparable in spirit to **NBA 2K-style broadcast polish**, while remaining realistic in v1 production scope.

---

# Platform / Tech Direction

## Shipping Strategy
The project has **two delivery phases**:

### Phase A: Proof of Concept
Target:
- **Windows**
- **macOS**
- **browser-delivered WebGL prototype**

Purpose:
- validate the handler-dog core loop
- validate command readability and dog response
- validate obstacle traversal, scoring, and replay logic at reduced fidelity
- validate investor/publisher/demo viability

### Phase B: Production 1.0
Target engine:
- **Unity3D**

Target platforms for 1.0:
- **Windows**
- **macOS**
- future console path should be preserved in architecture, but not required for first proof-of-concept planning unless explicitly noted as a later production consideration

## Technical Assumptions
Assume a modern Unity production stack, such as:
- C# gameplay systems
- ScriptableObjects for data-driven content
- Unity Input System
- Nav/pathing + custom local steering
- Timeline/Cinemachine for replay/camera direction
- Addressables/content streaming where appropriate
- Wwise or Unity audio stack, with clear recommendation
- platform abstraction for saves/cloud features
- deterministic-enough replay reconstruction strategy where feasible

You may recommend Unity package/tool choices, but keep them realistic and production-proven.

---

# Creative / Audio / Dialogue Direction

## Commentary / Backstory / Interview Content
Use **Eastworld** as the designated narrative/dialogue generation layer for:
- commentator dialogue generation pipelines
- color commentary variants
- handler backstories
- pre-event interview text
- post-run interview text
- venue flavor copy
- rivalry/framing text where appropriate

Treat Eastworld as a **content-generation and narrative-support layer**, not as a runtime replacement for core gameplay logic.

## Voice Generation
Use **ElevenLabs** as the TTS/voice generation solution for:
- play-by-play commentary
- color commentary
- PA announcer
- handler profile/interview narration if needed
- placeholder and/or scalable VO pipeline support

Assume final content may combine:
- authored scripts
- templated line assembly
- generated variants
- recorded or synthesized delivery

The PRD should specify how generated text is reviewed, approved, localized if needed, and converted into production VO assets.

---

# Core Product Vision

## Player Fantasy
The player is the **handler**, not the dog.

The gameplay fantasy is:
- reading the course
- moving with urgency and precision
- shaping the dog’s line through body position and motion
- issuing timely commands
- managing pressure, risk, and flow under judged competition conditions

The dog must feel like a **smart, fast, athletic partner**, not a puppet and not a generic follower.

The dog should demonstrate:
- momentum
- commitment
- obstacle reading
- contextual reaction to handler position
- breed-specific tendencies
- command interpretation with timing sensitivity
- recovery behavior after imperfect input

---

# Core Gameplay Requirements

## Handler Control
Player controls the handler in third-person with:
- analog locomotion
- sprint
- directional lean/body-language influence
- gesture inputs
- contextual command inputs
- optional voice command input via device microphone

Example commands include:
- come bye
- away
- jump
- tunnel
- weave
- table
- here
- out
- go

Commands must be interpreted contextually using:
- handler position
- handler facing
- handler velocity
- current obstacle opportunities
- dog state
- timing window
- recent command history

## Dog AI
Dog AI must be a primary gameplay system.

Required qualities:
- state-driven behavior with modular decision architecture
- perception of handler position, motion, facing, gesture, and command
- obstacle opportunity evaluation
- commitment logic
- momentum-aware pathing
- breed-based tuning
- recovery/fallback logic
- enough predictability for mastery, enough autonomy for realism

Example breed differentiation:
- Border Collie: higher acceleration, high speed, wider handling tolerance
- Sheltie: tighter turning, lower top speed, sharper responsiveness

## Obstacles / Course Types
Support at minimum:

### Standard Obstacles
- A-Frame
- Dog Walk
- Teeter
- Tunnel
- Weave Poles
- Pause Table
- Bar Jumps
- Tire Jump
- Broad Jump
- Wall Jump

### Course Types
- Standard
- Jumpers With Weaves

Jumpers With Weaves excludes:
- contact obstacles
- pause table

## Faults / Adjudication
The run must support authentic-seeming fault evaluation, including:
- missed contact
- wrong course
- refusal
- run-out
- time faults
- flow-sensitive run evaluation where applicable to presentation and performance analysis

## Championship Round
Include a modified **Time-to-Beat** format with:
- Dog Walk included
- escalating pressure presentation
- five height divisions

## Modes
At minimum:
- Training
- Single-run exhibition
- Career

Career should include:
- unlockable handlers
- unlockable dogs
- unlockable venues
- progression structure
- persistent performance records

---

# Presentation Requirements

## Camera
The game requires a premium sports-broadcast camera layer including:
- primary gameplay follow camera
- wide arena tracking camera
- cinematic chase camera
- replay camera network
- overhead angle
- side-on angle
- dog POV option
- freeze-frame highlight moments

## Replay
Replay is a core differentiator.

Required:
- super slow-motion replay on every fault
- replay on personal-best split
- multiple replay angles
- contextual replay framing
- post-run replay review/editor planning

## Crowd
The arena should feel alive and televised.

Required:
- 200+ spectator target for premium presentation mode, with scalable fallback
- procedural population strategy
- reactive cheering/ovation logic
- fault/tension reactions
- broadcast-style cutaways and hero shots

---

# Audio Requirements

## Broadcast Roles
Include:
- play-by-play commentator
- color analyst
- PA announcer

## Commentary Triggering
Commentary system must support:
- contextual trigger rules
- weighted/random line selection
- cooldowns
- anti-repetition logic
- breed callouts
- split-time callouts
- near-miss recognition
- fault reactions
- championship pressure escalation

## SFX / Mix
Include:
- spatial dog movement and footfalls
- obstacle impacts
- crowd layers tied to performance
- environmental ambience
- subtle music bed
- swelling victory theme
- dynamic mix priorities during replay and commentary

---

# Production Constraints

The package must preserve the premium vision while staying realistic for a first shippable version.

Prioritize:
1. handler-dog control loop
2. obstacle traversal and fault correctness
3. timing/scoring reliability
4. replay and readability
5. premium presentation layers
6. progression and metagame polish

Favor:
- modular architecture
- data-driven systems
- C# core systems with designer-friendly extension points
- tooling for iteration
- telemetry hooks
- scalable performance budgets

Avoid:
- vague aspirational language
- fake implementation detail
- overbuilt live-service assumptions
- unnecessary multiplayer complexity in v1
- treating the dog as a simple follower

---

# Deliverables

Produce the output as a **multi-document PRD package** in the exact order below.

Use **GitHub Flavored Markdown**.

Use:
- numbered headings
- concise but concrete bullet points
- tables where useful
- explicit acceptance criteria
- implementation recommendations
- realistic milestones

Be detailed enough that the team can break the output directly into epics and tickets.

---

## 1. Executive PRD
Length target: approximately 1 to 2 pages equivalent.

Include:
- game pillars
- target audience
- differentiators
- success metrics
  - gameplay
  - technical
  - engagement/business
- explicit non-goals / v1 exclusions

---

## 2. Feature Requirements by System
For each system below, include:
- Overview
- User stories
- Functional requirements
- Non-functional requirements
- Dependencies
- Edge cases / failure states
- Acceptance criteria

### Systems
1. Handler controls and input, including optional voice input
2. Dog AI, including command interpretation and breed traits
3. Obstacles and course authoring
4. Timing, scoring, and fault adjudication
5. Camera and replay director
6. UI / HUD
7. Audio system
8. Commentary system
9. Crowd system
10. Progression / career / unlock systems
11. Online features, including saves, leaderboards, and ghost runs if appropriate
12. Accessibility systems and settings
13. Performance / optimization / platform constraints

---

## 3. Unity Technical Architecture
Provide a production-oriented technical blueprint.

Include:
- proposed assembly/module breakdown
- major gameplay systems
- key classes/components/managers
- ScriptableObject/data schema recommendations
- scene/runtime flow
- event flow from countdown to results
- replay system architecture
- save/load strategy
- online architecture stance
- proof-of-concept architecture vs production 1.0 architecture where they differ

Include concrete class examples such as:
- `HandlerController`
- `DogAgentController`
- `CommandBuffer`
- `AgilityScoringService`
- `ObstacleBase`
- `ReplayDirector`
- `CommentaryDirector`
- `CrowdReactionSystem`
- `CareerProgressionService`

Explain responsibilities clearly.

---

## 4. AI and Physics Deep Spec
Go deep on the dog simulation and gameplay readability layer.

Include:
- top-level AI state model
- behavior/task structure
- command interpretation rules
- obstacle commitment logic
- recovery logic
- pathing approach comparison and recommendation
- local steering design
- contact detection design
- obstacle affordance modeling
- breed tuning parameters
- formulas or parameter tables where useful
- debugging hooks for AI and course-flow validation

---

## 5. Content and Tooling Plan
Include:
- course authoring workflow
- obstacle validation tooling
- sequence legality tools
- command timing visualization
- dog path debug tools
- replay diagnostics
- commentary authoring pipeline
- Eastworld content generation workflow
- ElevenLabs VO pipeline workflow
- telemetry hooks for balancing and usability

Make a clear distinction between:
- v1 tooling
- later tooling

---

## 6. Production Plan
Create a realistic staged roadmap.

Include:
- milestone table
- duration estimates
- team assumptions
- key deliverables
- exit criteria

Use stages such as:
1. foundation and technical spike
2. proof-of-concept handler/dog loop
3. obstacles and adjudication
4. full run loop and replay
5. presentation/crowd/commentary layer
6. progression and content expansion
7. optimization and platform hardening
8. 1.0 ship prep

Also include:
- small strike-team version
- fuller production-team version
- risk register with top 10 risks
- QA strategy
- validation matrix
- performance budgets
- browser/WebGL proof-of-concept constraints
- migration/pivot considerations from WebGL prototype to Unity 1.0 production architecture

---

## 7. Open Questions and Decision Log
List the major unresolved questions requiring stakeholder decisions.

Include examples such as:
- exact scope of Eastworld-generated content
- approval workflow for generated scripts
- ElevenLabs licensing and voice ownership workflow
- recorded VO vs synthesized VO split
- mocap scope
- breed count at launch
- number of launch venues
- ghost run scope in v1
- replay storage format
- whether browser proof-of-concept shares code with production branch or is a separate prototype track

For each question:
- explain why it matters
- recommend a default decision if unanswered

---

# Output Standards

Your final PRD package must be:
- production-oriented
- technically credible
- specific
- modular
- realistic for v1
- extensible for future premium polish

Do not write like marketing copy.
Do not hand-wave dog AI.
Do not hand-wave replay implementation.
Do not omit the premium sports presentation layer.
Do not optimize for elegance at the expense of execution clarity.

Where tradeoffs exist:
- make a recommendation
- explain why
- preserve future extensibility

Write as if this will be used to start pre-production immediately.</div></div> <div class="flex gap-1.5 justify-end text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-500"> <div aria-label="Edit" class="flex"><button <span class="text-sm font-medium leading-6 opacity-80  capitalize">Thought Process</span> <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="size-4 transition-transform duration-200 opacity-60"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="m8.25 4.5 7.5 7.5-7.5 7.5"></path></svg></button> </div></div> <div class="w-full overflow-hidden h-0"><div class="thinking-block w-full h-full flex pl-1.5 gap-2 text-[#0d0d0d] dark:text-white max-w-4/5"><div class="flex flex-col items-center mt-0 mx-2"><div class="min-h-2.5 flex-1 w-[1px] border-l-1 border-[#0d0d0d]/12 dark:border-[#fff]/12"></div></div> <blockquote slot="content" dir="auto" class="text-sm leading-6 text-black/40 dark:text-white/40"><p dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">This is a substantial request for a comprehensive PRD package for a premium dog agility sports simulation game. Let me break down what's being asked: </p><div class=""></div><ol start="1" dir="auto" data-emit-thinking-stage="false" style="padding-left: calc(var(--spacing, .25rem) * 4);"><li class="text-start">Executive PRD (1-2 pages) </li><li class="text-start">Feature Requirements by System (13 systems) </li><li class="text-start">Unity Technical Architecture </li><li class="text-start">AI and Physics Deep Spec </li><li class="text-start">Content and Tooling Plan </li><li class="text-start">Production Plan </li><li class="text-start">Open Questions and Decision Log </li></ol><div class=""></div><p dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">The user wants this to be production-ready, technically detailed, and realistic for a v1 ship. They want specific class names, implementation details, and acceptance criteria. </p><div class=""></div><p dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">Let me approach this systematically, creating each document with the depth and specificity required. I'll need to: </p><div class=""></div><ol start="1" dir="auto" data-emit-thinking-stage="false" style="padding-left: calc(var(--spacing, .25rem) * 4);"><li class="text-start">Think about the core handler-dog gameplay loop </li><li class="text-start">Design realistic AI systems for the dog </li><li class="text-start">Create proper technical architecture </li><li class="text-start">Define realistic production milestones </li><li class="text-start">Consider the commentary/audio systems </li><li class="text-start">Address browser vs production builds </li></ol><div class=""></div><p dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">Key technical considerations: </p><ul dir="auto"><li class="text-start"> Handler controls the human, not the dog</li><li class="text-start"> Dog is AI-driven with systemic behavior</li><li class="text-start"> Commands are interpreted contextually</li><li class="text-start"> Momentum and physics matter</li><li class="text-start"> Authentic agility rules and faulting</li><li class="text-start"> Broadcast presentation quality</li> </ul><div class=""></div><p dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">Let me start writing this comprehensive PRD package, ensuring I follow the exact document structure requested and use GitHub Flavored Markdown with proper formatting. </p><div class=""></div><p dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">I'll write this as if I'm a AAA game engineering lead creating documents that will actually be used by a production team. The tone should be technical, specific, and implementation-oriented. </p></blockquote></div> </div><h1 dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">Westminster Agility Masters: Handler's Edge </h1><h2 dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">Engineering PRD Package </h2><hr class="border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#EBEBEB]/16"><h1 dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">1. Executive PRD </h1><h2 dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">1.1 Product Overview </h2><p dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c"><strong>Westminster Agility Masters: Handler's Edge</strong> is a premium dog agility sports simulation where the player controls the <strong>handler</strong> in third-person perspective. The <strong>dog</strong> is an AI-driven athletic partner with systemic intelligence, momentum-aware movement, and breed-specific behaviors. </p><div class=""></div><p dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">This is a sports-sim, not an arcade pet game. The gameplay fantasy centers on reading courses, positioning, timing commands, and managing risk under judged competition conditions. </p><div class=""></div><hr class="border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#EBEBEB]/16"><h2 dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">1.2 Game Pillars </h2><div class="relative w-full group/table mb-5"><div class="overflow-x-auto relative w-full scrollbar-hidden"><table class="min-w-full text-sm text-left text-gray-500 w-fit dark:text-gray-400"><thead class="text-gray-700 uppercase border-none dark:text-gray-400"><tr class="border-none bg-[#f7f7f7] dark:bg-[#F7F7F70F]"><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">Pillar</div></div> </th><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">Description</div></div> </th></tr></thead> <tbody><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null"><strong>Handler-First Control</strong></td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Player controls the human, not the dog. Mastery comes from positioning, timing, and communication.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null"><strong>Believable Dog Intelligence</strong></td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog is a smart athletic partner with momentum, commitment logic, and breed-specific tendencies. Predictable enough for mastery, autonomous enough for realism.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null"><strong>Authentic Adjudication</strong></td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Real fault evaluation, timing, and scoring. Competition rules matter and are visibly enforced.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null"><strong>Broadcast Presentation</strong></td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Sports-broadcast quality cameras, replays, commentary, and crowd energy. Every run should look telegenic.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null"><strong>Replay Spectacle</strong></td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Slow-motion highlights, multi-angle review, and shareable moments are core to the experience.</td> class="border-none bg-[#f7f7f7] dark:bg-[#F7F7F70F]"><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">Differentiator</div></div> </th><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">Competitor Gap</div></div> </th></tr></thead> <tbody><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Handler control (not dog control)</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">No competitor offers this; all existing dog agility games control the dog directly.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Systemic dog AI with breed traits</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Competitors use simple pathfinding or puppet-style control.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Authentic fault adjudication</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Competitors simplify or omit fault systems.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Broadcast replay system</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Competitors lack replay depth and multi-angle presentation.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Commentary + crowd reactivity</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Competitors treat presentation as afterthought.</td> head-to-head</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Scope risk; border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Budget risk; v1 uses procedural + keyframe blend; mocap for sequel.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Voice chat or social features</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Not core to handler-dog fantasy.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Mobile platform</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Controls and presentation target PC/console; mobile is separate product consideration.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Real-money transactions</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Premium v1 product; no MTX.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">VR mode</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Significant UX and tech risk; future consideration.</td> </tr></tbody></table></div> <button class="absolute top-1 right-1 class="svelte-121hp7c">Phase A: Proof of Concept </h3><p dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c"><strong>Platforms:</strong> Windows, macOS, WebGL (browser) </p><div class=""></div><p dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c"><strong>Scope:</strong> </p><ul dir="auto"><li class="text-start"> Single handler + single dog</li><li class="text-start"> 3-5 obstacle types</li><li class="text-start"> 2 course layouts</li><li class="text-start"> Basic command system</li><li class="text-start"> Simple timing and fault detection</li><li class="text-start"> Single camera mode</li><li class="text-start"> No commentary, minimal crowd</li><li class="text-start"> Save/load not required</li> </ul><div class=""></div><p dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c"><strong>Exit Criteria:</strong> </p><ul dir="auto"><li class="text-start"> Handler-dog loop validated as fun and readable</li><li class="text-start"> Command timing feels skill-based</li><li class="text-start"> Dog behavior is predictable enough for mastery</li><li class="text-start"> Stakeholder/investor demo viable</li> </ul><div class=""></div><h3 dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">Phase B: Production 1.0 </h3><p dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c"><strong>Platforms:</strong> Windows, macOS (console architecture preserved) </p><div class=""></div><p dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c"><strong>Scope:</strong> </p><ul dir="auto"><li class="text-start"> Full obstacle set</li><li class="text-start"> Multiple handlers and dogs with breed differentiation</li><li class="text-start"> Career mode with progression</li><li class="text-start"> Full commentary system</li><li class="text-start"> Crowd and presentation layers</li><li class="text-start"> Replay system</li><li class="text-start"> Full adjudication</li><li class="text-start"> Accessibility features</li><li class="text-start"> Localization foundation</li> </ul><div class=""></div><hr class="border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#EBEBEB]/16"><h1 dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">2. Feature Requirements by System </h1><hr class="border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#EBEBEB]/16"><h2 dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">2.1 Handler Controls and Input System </h2><h3 dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">2.1.1 Overview </h3><p dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">The handler control system is the player's primary interface with the game. Players control a human handler in third-person view, using analog locomotion, gestures, and contextual commands to direct their dog partner through agility courses. </p><div class=""></div><p dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c"><strong>Key Design Principle:</strong> The handler never has direct control over the dog. All influence is mediated through positioning, body language, and commands that the dog interprets based on its AI state, training level, and breed characteristics. </p><div class=""></div><hr class="border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#EBEBEB]/16"><h3 dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">2.1.2 User Stories </h3><div class="relative w-full group/table mb-5"><div class="overflow-x-auto relative w-full scrollbar-hidden"><table class="min-w-full text-sm text-left text-gray-500 w-fit dark:text-gray-400"><thead class="text-gray-700 uppercase border-none dark:text-gray-400"><tr class="border-none bg-[#f7f7f7] dark:bg-[#F7F7F70F]"><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">ID</div></div> </th><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">User Story</div></div> </th></tr></thead> <tbody><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-001</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">As a player, I want to move my handler with smooth analog control so I can position myself precisely on course.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-002</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">As a player, I want to sprint when needed so I can reach critical handling positions in time.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-003</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">As a player, I want to use arm gestures that the dog can read so I can influence direction at range.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-004</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">As a player, I want to issue verbal commands so I can direct the dog to specific obstacles.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-005</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">As a player, I want my body facing to influence dog interpretation so I can shape lines naturally.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-006</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">As a player, I want optional microphone voice input so I can use real voice commands for immersion.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-007</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">As a player, I want clear visual feedback on command recognition so I know what the dog perceived.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-008</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">As a player, I want input latency to be minimal so timing feels precise.</td> </tr></tbody></table></div> <button Requirements </h3><h4 dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">2.1.3.1 Locomotion </h4><div class="relative w-full group/table mb-5"><div class="overflow-x-auto relative w-full scrollbar-hidden"><table class="min-w-full text-sm text-left text-gray-500 w-fit dark:text-gray-400"><thead class="text-gray-700 uppercase border-none dark:text-gray-400"><tr class="border-none bg-[#f7f7f7] dark:bg-[#F7F7F70F]"><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">ID</div></div> </th><th scope="col" class="px-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-F001</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Handler must support 8-directional analog movement with full analog speed range (0-1 normalized).</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">P0</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-F002</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Handler must support sprint modifier that increases maximum speed by 60-80% (tunable).</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">P0</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-F003</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Handler must support directional lean input that biases body orientation without full rotation.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-F004</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Handler must accelerate and decelerate with realistic human momentum (not instant velocity changes).</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">P0</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-F005</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Handler must animate appropriately for current velocity and facing (8-way blended animation).</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Handler must not clip through obstacle boundaries or course barriers.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">ID</div></div> </th><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-NF001</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Input polling must occur at application frame rate; no frame-delayed input processing.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-NF002</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Input-to-visible-response latency must not exceed 100ms for locomotion, 200ms for dog command interpretation.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-NF003</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Input bindings must be fully rebindable via settings UI.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-NF004</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Default bindings must match industry-standard sports game layouts (left stick move, right stick camera, triggers for gestures).</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-NF005</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">All inputs must be compatible with keyboard + mouse, gamepad, and accessibility peripherals.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-NF006</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Input system must support Unity Input System package (not legacy input).</td> class="break-normal shrink-0">Resolution</div></div> </th></tr></thead> <tbody><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Handler runs off-course</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Invisible barriers prevent exit; handler is constrained to course boundary.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Handler collides with obstacle</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Handler slides along obstacle collision; no stuck states.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Handler sprints into dog</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">No collision between handler and dog (non-physical); handler passes through or dog dodges.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Command issued while dog committed</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog AI acknowledges but may not act; command queue behavior defined by dog state.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Voice input unrecognized</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Display "unclear" indicator; no command issued.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Input device disconnected mid-run</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Pause prompt; allow reconnection or controller swap.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Multiple commands rapid-fire</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Commands queue with timestamp; dog AI processes in order with timing windows.</td> </tr></tbody></table></div> <button class="absolute top-1 right-1 z-10 p-1.5 rounded-lg group/table mb-5"><div class="overflow-x-auto relative w-full scrollbar-hidden"><table class="min-w-full text-sm text-left text-gray-500 w-fit dark:text-gray-400"><thead class="text-gray-700 uppercase class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-AC001</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Handler locomotion is smooth and responsive with analog input.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Manual playtest, 10+ users confirm feel is acceptable.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-AC002</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Sprint provides noticeable speed increase without feeling unnatural.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Timing test: handler crosses course in measurably less time when sprinting.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-AC003</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Gestures produce visible handler animation and dog perception event.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Debug visualization confirms gesture perception by dog AI.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-AC004</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">All 9 commands can be issued and are visible to player.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Manual test each command; visual confirmation.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-AC005</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog responds to commands within defined timing window.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Automated test: issue command, verify dog state change within 200ms.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-AC006</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Voice input (if implemented) recognizes commands with ≥80% accuracy in quiet environment.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Automated test suite with sample audio files.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">H-AC007</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Input latency is within spec.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Instrumented test: measure time from input event to game state change.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">All inputs can be rebound in settings.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border 1.87109C15.6541 1.90851 16.0599 1.98788 16.4365 2.17969L16.6553 2.30176C17.151 2.60585 17.5548 3.04244 17.8203 3.56348L17.8867 3.70606C18.031 4.04235 18.0962 4.40306 18.1289 4.80371C18.1657 5.25395 18.165 5.81091 18.165 6.5V9.33301Z" fill="currentColor"></path></svg></button>  </div><hr class="border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#EBEBEB]/16"><h2 dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">2.2 Dog AI System </h2><h3 dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">2.2.1 Overview </h3><p dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">The dog AI is the primary gameplay system. The dog must behave as an intelligent athletic partner with: </p><ul dir="auto"><li class="text-start"> Momentum-aware movement</li><li class="text-start"> Obstacle evaluation and commitment</li><li class="text-start"> Handler perception and command interpretation</li><li class="text-start"> Breed-specific behavioral tendencies</li><li class="text-start"> Recovery logic for imperfect inputs</li><li class="text-start"> Enough predictability for player mastery</li> </ul><div class=""></div><p dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c"><strong>Critical Design Principle:</strong> The dog is not a puppet. The dog has agency, makes decisions, and can disagree with the handler. The player's skill is in communicating effectively, not controlling directly. </p><div class=""></div><hr class="border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#EBEBEB]/16"><h3 dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">2.2.2 User Stories </h3><div class="relative w-full group/table mb-5"><div class="overflow-x-auto relative w-full scrollbar-hidden"><table class="min-w-full text-sm text-left text-gray-500 w-fit dark:text-gray-400"><thead class="text-gray-700 uppercase border-none dark:text-gray-400"><tr class="border-none bg-[#f7f7f7] dark:bg-[#F7F7F70F]"><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">ID</div></div> </th><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">User Story</div></div> </th></tr></thead> <tbody><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-001</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">As a player, I want the dog to move with realistic momentum so lines and turns feel natural.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-002</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">As a player, I want the dog to perceive my position and facing so body language matters.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-003</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">As a player, I want the dog to interpret my commands in context so timing is skill-based.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-004</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">As a player, I want the dog to commit to obstacles realistically so I can trust or challenge its line.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-005</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">As a player, I want breed differences to matter so dog selection is meaningful.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-006</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">As a player, I want the dog to recover gracefully from my mistakes so runs don't end abruptly.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-007</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">As a player, I want to see what the dog is thinking so I can learn and improve.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-008</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">As a player, I want the dog to occasionally "refuse" unrealistic requests so authenticity is preserved.</td> </tr></tbody></table></div> <button class="absolute top-1 right-1 z-10 p-1.5 rounded-lg bg-[#f7f7f7] dark:bg-[#272A2F] hover:bg-[#E5E5E5] dark:hover:bg-[#272A2F] opacity-0 group-hover/table:opacity-100 transition-opacity duration-200"><svg class="size-4" stroke-width="1.677" viewBox="0 0 20 20" class="relative w-full group/table mb-5"><div class="overflow-x-auto relative w-full scrollbar-hidden"><table class="min-w-full text-sm text-left text-gray-500 w-fit dark:text-gray-400"><thead class="text-gray-700 uppercase border-none dark:text-gray-400"><tr class="border-none bg-[#f7f7f7] dark:bg-[#F7F7F70F]"><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">ID</div></div> </th><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">Requirement</div></div> </th><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">Priority</div></div> </th></tr></thead> <tbody><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-F001</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog must track handler position, velocity, and facing at all times.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">P0</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-F002</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog must track all obstacles within perception radius (configurable, default 15m).</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">P0</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-F003</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog must perceive handler gestures (arm extension, pointing, sweeping).</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">P0</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-F004</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog must receive and queue handler commands with timestamps.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">P0</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-F005</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog must have configurable "perception lag" to simulate reaction time (0-100ms).</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">P1</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-F006</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog must maintain spatial awareness of course boundaries.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">P0</td> </tr></tbody></table></div> <button class="absolute top-1 right-1 z-10 p-1.5 rounded-lg bg-[#f7f7f7] states: Idle, StartWait, Running, ObstacleApproach, ObstacleExecution, ObstacleExit, Search, Hesitate, Fault, Finish.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">P0</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog must transition states based on course position, handler state, command history, and obstacle proximity.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">State transitions must be logged for replay and debugging.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">P0</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td dark:text-gray-400"><thead class="text-gray-700 uppercase border-none dark:text-gray-400"><tr class="border-none bg-[#f7f7f7] dark:bg-[#F7F7F70F]"><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">ID</div></div> </th><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">Requirement</div></div> </th><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">Priority</div></div> </th></tr></thead> <tbody><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-F020</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog must interpret commands based on: command type, handler position, handler facing, handler velocity, current obstacle opportunities, current dog state, time since command, recent command history.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">P0</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-F021</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog must weight command interpretation by "training level" (dog skill attribute).</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">P1</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] null">D-F022</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog must have a command "confidence" value that affects response probability.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">P1</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-F023</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog must ignore or refuse commands that are physically impossible (e.g., wrong direction while committed).</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">P0</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-F024</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog must support directional commands (Come Bye, Away) that are interpreted relative to handler facing.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">P0</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-F025</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog must support object commands (Jump, Tunnel, Weave, Table) that resolve to the nearest valid instance.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">P0</td> </tr><tr style="text-align: null">D-F026</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog must support directional-object combinations (e.g., "Out Tunnel" = take the tunnel farther from current position).</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border handlerProximityPreference, commandResponseSpeed, faultTolerance, obstaclePreferenceWeights.</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Border Collie breed must have: high top speed (8.5 m/s), high acceleration (6 m/s²), wide turn radius at dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Shetland Sheepdog breed must have: moderate top speed (7 m/s), moderate acceleration (5 m/s²), tight turn radius, narrow class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">ID</div></div> </th><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog AI decision cycle must complete within 16ms (one frame at 60fps).</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-NF002</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog AI must be deterministic for replay reconstruction given identical inputs.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-NF003</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog AI must support debug visualization: state, target obstacle, approach path, command queue, perception radius.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">D-NF005</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog AI must be unit-testable without full scene </div><hr class="border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#EBEBEB]/16"><h3 dir="auto" class="svelte-121hp7c">2.2.6 Edge Cases / Failure States </h3><div class="relative w-full group/table mb-5"><div class="overflow-x-auto relative w-full scrollbar-hidden"><table class="min-w-full text-sm text-left text-gray-500 w-fit dark:text-gray-400"><thead class="text-gray-700 uppercase border-none dark:text-gray-400"><tr class="border-none bg-[#f7f7f7] dark:bg-[#F7F7F70F]"><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">Edge Case</div></div> </th><th scope="col" class="px-3! py-1.5!" style="text-align: null"><div class="gap-1.5 text-left"><div class="break-normal shrink-0">Resolution</div></div> </th></tr></thead> <tbody><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">No obstacles within perception</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog enters Search state, moves toward handler.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Multiple obstacles equally viable</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog uses breed preference weights; may choose "wrong" one if handler doesn't disambiguate.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Handler and dog on opposite sides of course</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog evaluates distance; may refuse distant obstacle.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Command conflicts with commitment</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog acknowledges command but does not act; may show hesitation.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog runs into obstacle (collision)</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Physics resolves; dog plays stumble/recovery animation.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog exits course boundary</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Immediate fault; dog returns to course.</td> </tr><tr class="border-b-1 border-[#d1d5dc] dark:border-gray-850"><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Extreme handler distance</td><td class="px-3! py-3! border-none text-gray-900 dark:text-white border border-[#EBEBEB] dark:border-[#FFFFFF0D] max-w-[500px] min-w-[180px]" style="text-align: null">Dog may refuse obstacles due to handler proximity preference.</td> </tr></tbody></table></div> <button class="absolute top-1 right-1 z-10 p-1.5 rounded-lg bg-[#f7f7f7] dark:bg-[#272A2F] hover:bg-[#E5E5E5] dark:hover:bg-[#272A2F] opacity-0
