Acceptance Criteria</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Criterion</th><th>Verification</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>H-AC001</td><td>Handler locomotion is smooth and responsive with analog input.</td><td>Manual playtest, 10+ users confirm feel is acceptable.</td></tr><tr><td>H-AC002</td><td>Sprint provides noticeable speed increase without feeling unnatural.</td><td>Timing test: handler crosses course in measurably less time when sprinting.</td></tr><tr><td>H-AC003</td><td>Gestures produce visible handler animation and dog perception event.</td><td>Debug visualization confirms gesture perception by dog AI.</td></tr><tr><td>H-AC004</td><td>All 9 commands can be issued and are visible to player.</td><td>Manual test each command; visual confirmation.</td></tr><tr><td>H-AC005</td><td>Dog responds to commands within defined timing window.</td><td>Automated test: issue command, verify dog state change within 200ms.</td></tr><tr><td>H-AC006</td><td>Voice input (if implemented) recognizes commands with ≥80% accuracy in quiet environment.</td><td>Automated test suite with sample audio files.</td></tr><tr><td>H-AC007</td><td>Input latency is within spec.</td><td>Instrumented test: measure time from input event to game state change.</td></tr><tr><td>H-AC008</td><td>All inputs can be rebound in settings.</td><td>Manual test: rebind all inputs, verify function.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h2>2.2 Dog AI System</h2><h3>2.2.1 Overview</h3><p>The dog AI is the primary gameplay system. The dog must behave as an intelligent athletic partner with:</p><ul><li>Momentum-aware movement</li><li>Obstacle evaluation and commitment</li><li>Handler perception and command interpretation</li><li>Breed-specific behavioral tendencies</li><li>Recovery logic for imperfect inputs</li><li>Enough predictability for player mastery</li></ul><p><strong>Critical Design Principle:</strong> The dog is not a puppet. The dog has agency, makes decisions, and can disagree with the handler. The player's skill is in communicating effectively, not controlling directly.</p><hr><h3>2.2.2 User Stories</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>User Story</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>D-001</td><td>As a player, I want the dog to move with realistic momentum so lines and turns feel natural.</td></tr><tr><td>D-002</td><td>As a player, I want the dog to perceive my position and facing so body language matters.</td></tr><tr><td>D-003</td><td>As a player, I want the dog to interpret my commands in context so timing is skill-based.</td></tr><tr><td>D-004</td><td>As a player, I want the dog to commit to obstacles realistically so I can trust or challenge its line.</td></tr><tr><td>D-005</td><td>As a player, I want breed differences to matter so dog selection is meaningful.</td></tr><tr><td>D-006</td><td>As a player, I want the dog to recover gracefully from my mistakes so runs don't end abruptly.</td></tr><tr><td>D-007</td><td>As a player, I want to see what the dog is thinking so I can learn and improve.</td></tr><tr><td>D-008</td><td>As a player, I want the dog to occasionally "refuse" unrealistic requests so authenticity is preserved.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.2.3 Functional Requirements</h3><h4>2.2.3.1 Perception</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>D-F001</td><td>Dog must track handler position, velocity, and facing at all times.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F002</td><td>Dog must track all obstacles within perception radius (configurable, default 15m).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F003</td><td>Dog must perceive handler gestures (arm extension, pointing, sweeping).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F004</td><td>Dog must receive and queue handler commands with timestamps.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F005</td><td>Dog must have configurable "perception lag" to simulate reaction time (0-100ms).</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>D-F006</td><td>Dog must maintain spatial awareness of course boundaries.</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.2.3.2 State Machine</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>D-F010</td><td>Dog must implement a hierarchical state machine with top-level states: Idle, StartWait, Running, ObstacleApproach, ObstacleExecution, ObstacleExit, Search, Hesitate, Fault, Finish.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F011</td><td>Dog must transition states based on course position, handler state, command history, and obstacle proximity.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F012</td><td>State transitions must be logged for replay and debugging.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F013</td><td>State machine must support breed-specific transition probabilities and timing modifiers.</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.2.3.3 Command Interpretation</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>D-F020</td><td>Dog must interpret commands based on: command type, handler position, handler facing, handler velocity, current obstacle opportunities, current dog state, time since command, recent command history.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F021</td><td>Dog must weight command interpretation by "training level" (dog skill attribute).</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>D-F022</td><td>Dog must have a command "confidence" value that affects response probability.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>D-F023</td><td>Dog must ignore or refuse commands that are physically impossible (e.g., wrong direction while committed).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F024</td><td>Dog must support directional commands (Come Bye, Away) that are interpreted relative to handler facing.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F025</td><td>Dog must support object commands (Jump, Tunnel, Weave, Table) that resolve to the nearest valid instance.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F026</td><td>Dog must support directional-object combinations (e.g., "Out Tunnel" = take the tunnel farther from current position).</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.2.3.4 Obstacle Engagement</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>D-F030</td><td>Dog must evaluate obstacles based on: distance, approach angle, handler position relative to obstacle, handler motion vector, current velocity, breed preferences.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F031</td><td>Dog must calculate an "approach path" to each visible obstacle.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F032</td><td>Dog must commit to an obstacle when within commitment distance (breed and speed dependent).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F033</td><td>Dog must refuse to re-evaluate obstacle choice after commitment unless fault or refusal occurs.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F034</td><td>Dog must adjust stride pattern when approaching obstacles (shorten strides for collection).</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>D-F035</td><td>Dog must have breed-specific approach angle tolerances (e.g., Border Collie wider, Sheltie tighter).</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.2.3.5 Movement and Physics</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>D-F040</td><td>Dog must move with momentum: acceleration, deceleration, and turning radius based on current speed.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F041</td><td>Dog must have breed-tuned parameters: top speed, acceleration, deceleration, turn rate, stride length, stride frequency.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F042</td><td>Dog must have realistic turning behavior: cannot instant-turn at speed; must decelerate or arc.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F043</td><td>Dog must animate appropriately for current gait: walk, trot, canter, gallop.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F044</td><td>Dog must play specific animations for obstacle entry, traversal, and exit.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F045</td><td>Dog must have collision with course boundaries and obstacles (non-traversable).</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.2.3.6 Breed Differentiation</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>D-F050</td><td>System must support breed data definitions with the following parameters: topSpeed, acceleration, deceleration, turnRadiusAtSpeed, strideLength, strideFrequency, approachAngleTolerance, commitmentDistance, handlerProximityPreference, commandResponseSpeed, faultTolerance, obstaclePreferenceWeights.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F051</td><td>Border Collie breed must have: high top speed (8.5 m/s), high acceleration (6 m/s²), wide turn radius at speed, generous approach angles, high handler sensitivity.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>D-F052</td><td>Shetland Sheepdog breed must have: moderate top speed (7 m/s), moderate acceleration (5 m/s²), tight turn radius, narrow approach angles, very high handler sensitivity, sharp responsiveness.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>D-F053</td><td>System must support at least 5 breed archetypes at v1 launch.</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.2.3.7 Recovery and Fault Behavior</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>D-F060</td><td>Dog must enter "Hesitate" state when command is unclear or conflicting.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F061</td><td>Dog must enter "Search" state when no clear obstacle target exists, circling toward handler.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>D-F062</td><td>Dog must have configurable "fault recovery speed" - time to return to runnable state after a fault.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>D-F063</td><td>Dog must visually communicate state (body language: ears, tail, head position).</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>D-F064</td><td>Dog must not enter unrecoverable states; all states must transition back to runnable within 5 seconds of correct handler input.</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.2.4 Non-Functional Requirements</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>D-NF001</td><td>Dog AI decision cycle must complete within 16ms (one frame at 60fps).</td></tr><tr><td>D-NF002</td><td>Dog AI must be deterministic for replay reconstruction given identical inputs.</td></tr><tr><td>D-NF003</td><td>Dog AI must support debug visualization: state, target obstacle, approach path, command queue, perception radius.</td></tr><tr><td>D-NF004</td><td>Dog AI parameters must be defined in ScriptableObjects for designer iteration.</td></tr><tr><td>D-NF005</td><td>Dog AI must be unit-testable without full scene context.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.2.5 Dependencies</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Dependency</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Handler Control System</td><td>Dog perceives handler state; requires handler position/velocity/facing data.</td></tr><tr><td>Obstacle System</td><td>Dog navigates obstacles; requires obstacle definitions and affordances.</td></tr><tr><td>Animation System</td><td>Dog state must map to animation states.</td></tr><tr><td>Physics System</td><td>Dog movement requires physics integration for momentum.</td></tr><tr><td>Scoring System</td><td>Dog behavior triggers fault events.</td></tr><tr><td>Course Authoring</td><td>Dog requires valid course layout.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.2.6 Edge Cases / Failure States</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Edge Case</th><th>Resolution</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>No obstacles within perception</td><td>Dog enters Search state, moves toward handler.</td></tr><tr><td>Multiple obstacles equally viable</td><td>Dog uses breed preference weights; may choose "wrong" one if handler doesn't disambiguate.</td></tr><tr><td>Handler and dog on opposite sides of course</td><td>Dog evaluates distance; may refuse distant obstacle.</td></tr><tr><td>Command conflicts with commitment</td><td>Dog acknowledges command but does not act; may show hesitation.</td></tr><tr><td>Dog runs into obstacle (collision)</td><td>Physics resolves; dog plays stumble/recovery animation.</td></tr><tr><td>Dog exits course boundary</td><td>Immediate fault; dog returns to course.</td></tr><tr><td>Extreme handler distance</td><td>Dog may refuse obstacles due to handler proximity preference.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.2.7 Acceptance Criteria</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Criterion</th><th>Verification</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>D-AC001</td><td>Dog moves with visible momentum; no instant direction changes at speed.</td><td>Visual inspection; velocity curve analysis.</td></tr><tr><td>D-AC002</td><td>Dog commits to obstacles at appropriate distance based on speed and breed.</td><td>Automated test: verify commitment distance within 0.5m of breed spec.</td></tr><tr><td>D-AC003</td><td>Dog responds to correctly-timed commands within 200ms.</td><td>Automated test: measure response time.</td></tr><tr><td>D-AC004</td><td>Breed differences are noticeable in gameplay.</td><td>A/B test: players can distinguish breeds by behavior.</td></tr><tr><td>D-AC005</td><td>Dog recovers from unclear state within 5 seconds of correct handler input.</td><td>Manual test: create confusion, verify recovery.</td></tr><tr><td>D-AC006</td><td>Dog AI decision cycle does not exceed 16ms.</td><td>Profiler verification.</td></tr><tr><td>D-AC007</td><td>Dog state debug visualization is accurate.</td><td>Visual debug + manual verification.</td></tr><tr><td>D-AC008</td><td>Dog behavior is deterministic for replay.</td><td>Automated test: run same inputs twice, verify identical output.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h2>2.3 Obstacles and Course Authoring System</h2><h3>2.3.1 Overview</h3><p>The obstacle and course authoring system defines the physical and logical elements that form agility courses. The system must support:</p><ul><li>All standard agility obstacle types</li><li>Course layout authoring</li><li>Obstacle sequence definition</li><li>Validation of course legality</li><li>Runtime obstacle affordances for dog AI</li></ul><hr><h3>2.3.2 User Stories</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>User Story</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>O-001</td><td>As a designer, I want to place obstacles on a course canvas so I can create course layouts.</td></tr><tr><td>O-002</td><td>As a designer, I want to define the required obstacle sequence so the game can evaluate faults.</td></tr><tr><td>O-003</td><td>As a designer, I want to validate course legality so players don't encounter impossible sequences.</td></tr><tr><td>O-004</td><td>As a player, I want obstacles to be visually distinct so I can identify them during gameplay.</td></tr><tr><td>O-005</td><td>As a player, I want contact obstacles to have visible contact zones so I can understand fault calls.</td></tr><tr><td>O-006</td><td>As a player, I want weave poles to be clearly numbered so I know entry direction.</td></tr><tr><td>O-007</td><td>As a designer, I want to tune obstacle parameters (height, spacing) so I can adjust difficulty.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.3.3 Functional Requirements</h3><h4>2.3.3.1 Obstacle Types</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>O-F001</td><td>System must support the following obstacle types: A-Frame, Dog Walk, Teeter, Tunnel (open and chute), Weave Poles (6 and 12 pole sets), Pause Table, Bar Jump, Tire Jump, Broad Jump, Wall Jump.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>O-F002</td><td>Each obstacle type must have a unique identifier, visual prefab, collision geometry, and affordance data.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>O-F003</td><td>Contact obstacles (A-Frame, Dog Walk, Teeter) must have defined contact zones with visual and logical presence.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>O-F004</td><td>Weave poles must support configurable pole count (standard: 6 or 12) and spacing (standard: 24 inches).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>O-F005</td><td>Tunnels must support straight and curved variants with configurable bend angle.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>O-F006</td><td>Jumps must support configurable height (based on dog height division).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>O-F007</td><td>Pause Table must have defined zone diameter and required pause duration.</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.3.3.2 Course Layout</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>O-F010</td><td>System must support placing obstacles on a rectangular course area (minimum 100ft x 100ft).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>O-F011</td><td>Each obstacle instance must have: position, rotation, scale (if applicable), sequence number.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>O-F012</td><td>System must support obstacle duplication, deletion, and transformation (move, rotate).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>O-F013</td><td>System must support course boundaries (ring rope or invisible barrier).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>O-F014</td><td>System must support start line and finish line markers.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>O-F015</td><td>System must support course surface type (grass, dirt, turf) which affects dog footing and audio.</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.3.3.3 Sequence Definition</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>O-F020</td><td>System must support defining a required obstacle sequence (dog must take obstacles in order).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>O-F021</td><td>System must support course types: Standard, Jumpers With Weaves.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>O-F022</td><td>Jumpers With Weaves courses must exclude contact obstacles and pause table by definition.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>O-F023</td><td>System must detect and flag wrong-course faults when dog takes obstacles out of sequence.</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.3.3.4 Obstacle Affordances</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>O-F030</td><td>Each obstacle must expose affordance data to dog AI: entry points, exit points, approach angles, traversal time estimate, commitment zone.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>O-F031</td><td>Obstacles must provide approach direction hints for dog AI pathfinding.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>O-F032</td><td>Weave poles must provide entry direction (left vs right) based on pole numbering.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>O-F033</td><td>Tunnels must provide entry and exit points for both ends.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>O-F034</td><td>Contact obstacles must provide up-ramp and down-ramp approach vectors.</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.3.3.5 Course Validation</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>O-F040</td><td>System must validate that all obstacles in sequence are reachable (no impossible layouts).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>O-F041</td><td>System must warn if obstacles are placed too close together (collision overlap).</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>O-F042</td><td>System must warn if approach angles between obstacles exceed breed capability.</td><td>P2</td></tr><tr><td>O-F043</td><td>System must validate that course fits within competition ring dimensions.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>O-F044</td><td>System must save validation state with course data.</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.3.4 Non-Functional Requirements</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>O-NF001</td><td>Course data must be stored as ScriptableObjects or JSON for serialization and version control.</td></tr><tr><td>O-NF002</td><td>Course loading must complete within 2 seconds for standard courses.</td></tr><tr><td>O-NF003</td><td>Obstacle prefabs must use LOD groups for performance (high, medium, low).</td></tr><tr><td>O-NF004</td><td>Course authoring tool must run in Unity Editor without play mode.</td></tr><tr><td>O-NF005</td><td>Course data format must be forward-compatible for future expansion.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.3.5 Dependencies</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Dependency</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Dog AI System</td><td>Dog AI requires obstacle affordances for navigation.</td></tr><tr><td>Scoring System</td><td>Scoring requires sequence data for fault detection.</td></tr><tr><td>Animation System</td><td>Obstacles require traversal animation triggers.</td></tr><tr><td>Audio System</td><td>Obstacles require impact and traversal sound effects.</td></tr><tr><td>Course Authoring Tool</td><td>Designer tooling for course creation.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.3.6 Edge Cases / Failure States</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Edge Case</th><th>Resolution</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Obstacles overlap</td><td>Collision detection prevents placement; designer must reposition.</td></tr><tr><td>Sequence has gaps</td><td>Validation warns designer; course can be saved but flagged as incomplete.</td></tr><tr><td>Obstacle outside boundary</td><td>Validation error; cannot save course until corrected.</td></tr><tr><td>Duplicate sequence numbers</td><td>Validation error; designer must assign unique sequence.</td></tr><tr><td>Impossible approach angle</td><td>Warning only; designer can override for challenge courses.</td></tr><tr><td>Missing start/finish</td><td>Validation error; required for valid course.</td></tr><tr><td>Weave pole entry direction ambiguous</td><td>Editor visualization shows required entry; designer can flip numbering.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.3.7 Acceptance Criteria</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Criterion</th><th>Verification</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>O-AC001</td><td>All 11 obstacle types can be placed and configured in course editor.</td><td>Manual test: place each type, verify configuration options.</td></tr><tr><td>O-AC002</td><td>Course validation correctly identifies all error conditions.</td><td>Automated test suite with invalid course definitions.</td></tr><tr><td>O-AC003</td><td>Course loads in gameplay within 2 seconds.</td><td>Instrumented test on minimum spec hardware.</td></tr><tr><td>O-AC004</td><td>Obstacle affordances correctly guide dog AI.</td><td>Dog AI test: verify dog approaches obstacles correctly.</td></tr><tr><td>O-AC005</td><td>Sequence faults are correctly detected during gameplay.</td><td>Test runs with intentional wrong-course actions.</td></tr><tr><td>O-AC006</td><td>Course data survives serialization round-trip.</td><td>Save/load test: verify all properties preserved.</td></tr><tr><td>O-AC007</td><td>Contact zones are visually distinct and functionally active.</td><td>Visual test + fault test: verify contact detection.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h2>2.4 Timing, Scoring, and Fault Adjudication System</h2><h3>2.4.1 Overview</h3><p>The scoring system provides authentic agility competition adjudication. It tracks time, evaluates faults, and produces final scores. The system must be authoritative, transparent, and aligned with real-world agility rules while providing clear feedback to players.</p><hr><h3>2.4.2 User Stories</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>User Story</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>S-001</td><td>As a player, I want accurate run timing so my performance is measured correctly.</td></tr><tr><td>S-002</td><td>As a player, I want clear fault calls so I understand what went wrong.</td></tr><tr><td>S-003</td><td>As a player, I want to see my split times so I can analyze my run.</td></tr><tr><td>S-004</td><td>As a player, I want the scoring system to match real agility rules so the game feels authentic.</td></tr><tr><td>S-005</td><td>As a player, I want to see fault replays so I can learn from mistakes.</td></tr><tr><td>S-006</td><td>As a player, I want time faults calculated correctly so course times are meaningful.</td></tr><tr><td>S-007</td><td>As a player, I want to see my final score and placement context after each run.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.4.3 Functional Requirements</h3><h4>2.4.3.1 Timing</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>S-F001</td><td>System must track run time from start line crossing to finish line crossing with centisecond precision (0.01s).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F002</td><td>System must record split times at each obstacle entry.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F003</td><td>System must calculate Standard Course Time (SCT) based on course length and difficulty.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F004</td><td>System must calculate time faults as: max(0, RunTime - SCT).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F005</td><td>System must display running timer during gameplay (optional: player can hide).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F006</td><td>System must record timestamps for all significant events (faults, commands, obstacle completions).</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.4.3.2 Fault Types</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>S-F010</td><td>System must detect and score the following fault types: Refusal/Run-out, Wrong Course, Missed Contact, Table Fault, Bar Knock, Exceeded Time Limit, Handler Contact with Dog, Handler Contact with Obstacle.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F011</td><td>Refusal: Dog stops or turns away from obstacle after commitment without completing it.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F012</td><td>Run-out: Dog passes obstacle entrance without taking it when it is the correct next obstacle.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F013</td><td>Wrong Course: Dog takes incorrect obstacle (wrong sequence number).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F014</td><td>Missed Contact: Dog exits contact obstacle without touching contact zone on descent.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F015</td><td>Table Fault: Dog leaves table before judge's release or fails to assume required position.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F016</td><td>Bar Knock: Dog displaces bar from jump cup.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F017</td><td>Time Faults: Each full second over SCT adds 1 fault.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F018</td><td>Maximum Course Time: Run terminates if dog exceeds maximum time limit (typically 2x SCT).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F019</td><td>Elimination: Three refusals or specific cumulative faults result in Elimination (non-qualifying score).</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.4.3.3 Score Calculation</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>S-F020</td><td>System must calculate final score as: Total Faults (course faults + time faults).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F021</td><td>Lower score is better; 0 faults = Clean Run.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F022</td><td>System must track YPS (Yards Per Second) = Course Length / Run Time.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>S-F023</td><td>System must provide placement context: 1st, 2nd, 3rd, etc. based on score, then time for tiebreakers.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>S-F024</td><td>System must differentiate qualifying runs (clean or below fault threshold) from non-qualifying.</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.4.3.4 Championship Round</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>S-F030</td><td>Championship round must implement Time-to-Beat format: dog with fastest clean run wins.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>S-F031</td><td>Championship must support five height divisions: 4", 8", 12", 16", 20" (or equivalent metric).</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>S-F032</td><td>Championship must include Dog Walk as mandatory obstacle.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>S-F033</td><td>Championship must escalate presentation pressure (crowd, commentary, camera) as run progresses.</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.4.3.5 Fault Feedback</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>S-F040</td><td>System must display fault call immediately when detected (text + audio).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F041</td><td>System must trigger slow-motion replay on any fault for player review.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F042</td><td>System must log all faults with timestamp, type, and position for post-run review.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>S-F043</td><td>System must differentiate "live" fault calls (during run) from post-run fault summary.</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.4.4 Non-Functional Requirements</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>S-NF001</td><td>Timing accuracy must be verified against known-good inputs (±0.01s tolerance).</td></tr><tr><td>S-NF002</td><td>Fault detection must be deterministic; same inputs produce same fault calls.</td></tr><tr><td>S-NF003</td><td>Scoring calculations must complete in under 1ms per event.</td></tr><tr><td>S-NF004</td><td>All scoring events must be serializable for replay and telemetry.</td></tr><tr><td>S-NF005</td><td>Score data must integrate with career progression and leaderboards.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.4.5 Dependencies</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Dependency</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Dog AI System</td><td>Fault detection requires dog state and position data.</td></tr><tr><td>Obstacle System</td><td>Fault detection requires obstacle state (bars, contacts, sequence).</td></tr><tr><td>Replay System</td><td>Fault replay requires integration with replay director.</td></tr><tr><td>Audio System</td><td>Fault calls require audio feedback.</td></tr><tr><td>UI System</td><td>Fault display requires HUD integration.</td></tr><tr><td>Commentary System</td><td>Commentary reacts to fault events.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.4.6 Edge Cases / Failure States</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Edge Case</th><th>Resolution</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Dog starts before countdown</td><td>False start; timer resets, run restarts.</td></tr><tr><td>Dog exits course boundary</td><td>Immediate elimination; run ends.</td></tr><tr><td>Multiple faults on same obstacle</td><td>Score most severe fault; do not double-count.</td></tr><tr><td>Fault on final obstacle</td><td>Run completes; fault scored normally.</td></tr><tr><td>Timer fails to start</td><td>Run invalid; prompt restart.</td></tr><tr><td>Simultaneous bar knock and wrong course</td><td>Score both faults; wrong course is more severe.</td></tr><tr><td>Dog refuses three times in sequence</td><td>Third refusal = elimination; run ends.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.4.7 Acceptance Criteria</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Criterion</th><th>Verification</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>S-AC001</td><td>Timer accuracy verified to ±0.01s over 60-second run.</td><td>Instrumented test with known timing intervals.</td></tr><tr><td>S-AC002</td><td>All fault types correctly detected in test scenarios.</td><td>Automated fault detection test suite.</td></tr><tr><td>S-AC003</td><td>Score calculation matches manual calculation for 20+ test runs.</td><td>Manual verification vs system output.</td></tr><tr><td>S-AC004</td><td>Fault replay triggers correctly and shows relevant moment.</td><td>Manual test: cause faults, verify replay.</td></tr><tr><td>S-AC005</td><td>Time-to-Beat format correctly determines winner.</td><td>Simulated championship test with multiple dogs.</td></tr><tr><td>S-AC006</td><td>Height divisions correctly applied to jump heights.</td><td>Test with dogs of different height classes.</td></tr><tr><td>S-AC007</td><td>Fault log is complete and accurate for post-run review.</td><td>Verify log matches observed faults.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h2>2.5 Camera and Replay Director System</h2><h3>2.5.1 Overview</h3><p>The camera and replay system provides broadcast-quality presentation. During gameplay, cameras follow handler and dog dynamically. After significant events and run completion, the replay system provides multi-angle, slow-motion review.</p><hr><h3>2.5.2 User Stories</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>User Story</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>C-001</td><td>As a player, I want the camera to follow my handler smoothly so I can see where I'm going.</td></tr><tr><td>C-002</td><td>As a player, I want multiple camera angles to choose from so I can find my preferred view.</td></tr><tr><td>C-003</td><td>As a player, I want automatic replay on faults so I can see what happened.</td></tr><tr><td>C-004</td><td>As a player, I want slow-motion replay so I can analyze timing.</td></tr><tr><td>C-005</td><td>As a player, I want to watch my full run replay after completion so I can review performance.</td></tr><tr><td>C-006</td><td>As a player, I want to scrub through replay with frame-by-frame control.</td></tr><tr><td>C-007</td><td>As a player, I want cinematic camera angles for highlights so my runs look professional.</td></tr><tr><td>C-008</td><td>As a player, I want dog POV camera option so I can see the course from the dog's perspective.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.5.3 Functional Requirements</h3><h4>2.5.3.1 Gameplay Cameras</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>C-F001</td><td>System must provide a primary third-person follow camera that tracks handler with smooth interpolation.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>C-F002</td><td>System must provide a wide arena tracking camera for full-course visibility.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>C-F003</td><td>System must provide a cinematic chase camera for dramatic moments.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>C-F004</td><td>System must provide an overhead camera angle.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>C-F005</td><td>System must provide a side-on tracking camera.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>C-F006</td><td>System must provide a dog POV camera option.</td><td>P2</td></tr><tr><td>C-F007</td><td>Camera must avoid clipping through obstacles and geometry.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>C-F008</td><td>Camera must maintain visibility of handler and dog at all times in follow modes.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>C-F009</td><td>Camera must support manual rotation control (right stick).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>C-F010</td><td>Camera must zoom out slightly when handler sprints to maintain framing.</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.5.3.2 Replay Recording</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>C-F020</td><td>System must record all gameplay events for replay: handler position, dog position, obstacle states, fault events, command events, timing data.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>C-F021</td><td>System must record at sufficient temporal resolution for smooth slow-motion (120Hz sampling minimum).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>C-F022</td><td>System must support replay of any recorded run with accurate reconstruction.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>C-F023</td><td>System must support variable playback speed: 0.1x, 0.25x, 0.5x, 1x, 2x.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>C-F024</td><td>System must support pause and frame-by-frame advance (forward and backward).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>C-F025</td><td>System must support scrubbing via timeline UI.</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.5.3.3 Automatic Replay Triggers</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>C-F030</td><td>System must trigger automatic slow-motion replay on any fault event.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>C-F031</td><td>System must trigger automatic replay on personal-best split achievement.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>C-F032</td><td>System must trigger automatic replay on clean run completion.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>C-F033</td><td>System must trigger replay on near-miss events (close fault that wasn't called).</td><td>P2</td></tr><tr><td>C-F034</td><td>Automatic replay must use the most dramatic relevant camera angle.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>C-F035</td><td>Automatic replay duration must be configurable (default: 5-8 seconds).</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.5.3.4 Replay Camera Network</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>C-F040</td><td>System must provide a network of virtual cameras positioned around the course.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>C-F041</td><td>System must automatically select best camera for each replay moment (or allow manual selection).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>C-F042</td><td>Camera options must include: hero angle (low, dramatic), wide angle (context), side angle (lateral motion), overhead angle (path visibility), dog close-up, handler close-up.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>C-F043</td><td>System must support smooth camera cuts between angles during replay.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>C-F044</td><td>System must freeze-frame on fault moment before replay playback.</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.5.3.5 Post-Run Replay Review</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>C-F050</td><td>System must provide full run replay after completion with timeline navigation.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>C-F051</td><td>System must highlight fault moments on timeline.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>C-F052</td><td>System must highlight split times on timeline.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>C-F053</td><td>System must allow saving replay clips to local storage (future: sharing).</td><td>P2</td></tr><tr><td>C-F054</td><td>System must support replay export to video file (PC/desktop only).</td><td>P2</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.5.4 Non-Functional Requirements</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>C-NF001</td><td>Replay recording must not impact gameplay frame rate (&gt;2fps overhead acceptable).</td></tr><tr><td>C-NF002</td><td>Replay reconstruction must be deterministic within visual tolerance.</td></tr><tr><td>C-NF003</td><td>Camera interpolation must be smooth (no visible jitter).</td></tr><tr><td>C-NF004</td><td>Replay scrubbing must respond within 100ms of input.</td></tr><tr><td>C-NF005</td><td>Camera system must integrate with Cinemachine for production-quality results.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.5.5 Dependencies</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Dependency</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Gameplay Systems</td><td>Replay requires all gameplay state data.</td></tr><tr><td>UI System</td><td>Replay controls require HUD integration.</td></tr><tr><td>Audio System</td><td>Replay requires audio time-stretch support.</td></tr><tr><td>Scoring System</td><td>Fault triggers require scoring event data.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.5.6 Edge Cases / Failure States</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Edge Case</th><th>Resolution</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Replay data corrupted</td><td>Discard replay; display "replay unavailable" message.</td></tr><tr><td>Camera occluded by geometry</td><td>Camera repositions automatically; fallback to wider angle.</td></tr><tr><td>Very long run (timeout)</td><td>Replay retains key moments; full replay may be truncated.</td></tr><tr><td>User skips replay mid-playback</td><td>Allow skip; resume gameplay or proceed to results.</td></tr><tr><td>Multiple events in quick succession</td><td>Queue replays; play in sequence or combine.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.5.7 Acceptance Criteria</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Criterion</th><th>Verification</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>C-AC001</td><td>All camera modes provide clear view of handler and dog.</td><td>Manual test: verify each camera angle.</td></tr><tr><td>C-AC002</td><td>Replay accurately reconstructs run within visual tolerance.</td><td>Compare live gameplay to replay frame-by-frame.</td></tr><tr><td>C-AC003</td><td>Slow-motion playback is smooth at 0.1x speed.</td><td>Visual inspection; no frame drops.</td></tr><tr><td>C-AC004</td><td>Automatic replay triggers correctly on all fault types.</td><td>Cause each fault type; verify replay triggers.</td></tr><tr><td>C-AC005</td><td>Camera does not clip through geometry in any mode.</td><td>Test on all course layouts.</td></tr><tr><td>C-AC006</td><td>Replay scrubbing is responsive.</td><td>Input latency test: &lt;100ms response.</td></tr><tr><td>C-AC007</td><td>Multiple camera angles available during replay.</td><td>Manual test: switch angles during replay.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h2>2.6 UI / HUD System</h2><h3>2.6.1 Overview</h3><p>The UI/HUD system provides all player-facing interface elements including gameplay HUD, menus, score displays, replay controls, and accessibility features. The design should feel like a broadcast sports presentation.</p><hr><h3>2.6.2 User Stories</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>User Story</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>U-001</td><td>As a player, I want to see the current time and fault count during my run.</td></tr><tr><td>U-002</td><td>As a player, I want to see the obstacle sequence so I know where to go next.</td></tr><tr><td>U-003</td><td>As a player, I want to see feedback when I issue commands.</td></tr><tr><td>U-004</td><td>As a player, I want clear post-run results with score breakdown.</td></tr><tr><td>U-005</td><td>As a player, I want accessible menus for game modes and settings.</td></tr><tr><td>U-006</td><td>As a player, I want HUD opacity and position to be configurable.</td></tr><tr><td>U-007</td><td>As a player, I want the UI to support my accessibility needs (colorblind, screen reader, etc.).</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.6.3 Functional Requirements</h3><h4>2.6.3.1 Gameplay HUD</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>U-F001</td><td>HUD must display: current run time (large, prominent), fault count, next obstacle indicator, dog state indicator, handler stamina (if applicable).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>U-F002</td><td>HUD must display command feedback: last command issued, command interpretation indicator (success/unclear/ignored).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>U-F003</td><td>HUD must display mini-map showing course layout with handler/dog positions and obstacle sequence.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>U-F004</td><td>HUD must display split times at each obstacle completion.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>U-F005</td><td>HUD must fade or minimize during cinematic moments.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>U-F006</td><td>HUD must be configurable: opacity, size, position, enable/disable individual elements.</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.6.3.2 Course Navigation</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>U-F010</td><td>System must display next obstacle indicator (arrow or highlight) in world space.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>U-F011</td><td>System must display obstacle sequence number on each obstacle.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>U-F012</td><td>System must highlight current obstacle (the one dog should take next).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>U-F013</td><td>Navigation aids must be togglable for difficulty settings (full aids, minimal aids, no aids).</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.6.3.3 Post-Run UI</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>U-F020</td><td>Post-run screen must display: final time, fault breakdown, final score, placement, qualification status.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>U-F021</td><td>Post-run screen must provide: replay button, retry button, return to menu button.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>U-F022</td><td>Post-run screen must display split time comparison to personal best (if applicable).</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>U-F023</td><td>Post-run screen must display animated score reveal (build tension).</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.6.3.4 Menus</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>U-F030</td><td>Main menu must include: Play (Training, Exhibition, Career), Settings, Credits, Exit.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>U-F031</td><td>Mode selection must clearly explain each mode.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>U-F032</td><td>Dog selection screen must display breed, stats, and visual preview.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>U-F033</td><td>Handler selection screen must display stats and visual preview.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>U-F034</td><td>Course selection screen must display course preview and difficulty rating.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>U-F035</td><td>All menus must support keyboard and gamepad navigation.</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.6.3.5 Accessibility</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>U-F040</td><td>UI must support colorblind modes (protanopia, deuteranopia, tritanopia).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>U-F041</td><td>UI must support high contrast mode.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>U-F042</td><td>UI must support scalable text and UI elements (75%-150%).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>U-F043</td><td>UI must support screen reader for menu navigation (where platform allows).</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>U-F044</td><td>UI must provide audio cues for all significant visual events.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>U-F045</td><td>UI must support configurable hold times for timed inputs.</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.6.4 Non-Functional Requirements</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>U-NF001</td><td>HUD must not obscure critical gameplay elements.</td></tr><tr><td>U-NF002</td><td>UI must respond to input within 50ms.</td></tr><tr><td>U-NF003</td><td>UI must render at consistent frame rate (no hitches during transitions).</td></tr><tr><td>U-NF004</td><td>UI must use Unity UI Toolkit or equivalent for resolution independence.</td></tr><tr><td>U-NF005</td><td>All text must support localization (Unicode, text sizing for translations).</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.6.5 Dependencies</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Dependency</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Scoring System</td><td>HUD displays scoring data.</td></tr><tr><td>Dog AI System</td><td>HUD displays dog state.</td></tr><tr><td>Obstacle System</td><td>HUD displays navigation aids.</td></tr><tr><td>Accessibility System</td><td>UI integrates with accessibility settings.</td></tr><tr><td>Localization System</td><td>All text requires localization support.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.6.6 Edge Cases / Failure States</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Edge Case</th><th>Resolution</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>HUD element overlaps</td><td>Configurable priority; less critical elements fade.</td></tr><tr><td>Screen aspect ratio extreme (21:9)</td><td>HUD anchors to safe areas; no stretching.</td></tr><tr><td>Resolution below minimum</td><td>Warning displayed; UI may appear cropped.</td></tr><tr><td>Localization text overflow</td><td>Text boxes expand; scroll if necessary.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.6.7 Acceptance Criteria</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Criterion</th><th>Verification</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>U-AC001</td><td>All HUD elements display correct data during gameplay.</td><td>Manual test: verify each element.</td></tr><tr><td>U-AC002</td><td>Navigation aids correctly indicate next obstacle.</td><td>Test on multiple courses.</td></tr><tr><td>U-AC003</td><td>Post-run screen shows accurate score breakdown.</td><td>Verify against scoring system output.</td></tr><tr><td>U-AC004</td><td>All menus are navigable with gamepad and keyboard.</td><td>Manual navigation test.</td></tr><tr><td>U-AC005</td><td>Colorblind modes correctly adjust all color-dependent elements.</td><td>Colorblind simulation test.</td></tr><tr><td>U-AC006</td><td>UI scaling works correctly at all supported resolutions.</td><td>Resolution test suite.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h2>2.7 Audio System</h2><h3>2.7.1 Overview</h3><p>The audio system provides immersive soundscapes including spatial audio for dog movement, obstacle interactions, crowd ambience, environmental sound, music, and commentary integration. The mix must be dynamic and broadcast-quality.</p><hr><h3>2.7.2 User Stories</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>User Story</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>A-001</td><td>As a player, I want to hear the dog's footfalls and breathing so I feel connected to my partner.</td></tr><tr><td>A-002</td><td>As a player, I want to hear obstacle impacts so I know when faults occur.</td></tr><tr><td>A-003</td><td>As a player, I want reactive crowd audio so the arena feels alive.</td></tr><tr><td>A-004</td><td>As a player, I want clear commentary that enhances the broadcast feel.</td></tr><tr><td>A-005</td><td>As a player, I want audio cues for faults so I don't have to look at the HUD.</td></tr><tr><td>A-006</td><td>As a player, I want configurable audio mix settings so I can balance elements.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.7.3 Functional Requirements</h3><h4>2.7.3.1 Dog Audio</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>A-F001</td><td>System must play spatialized footfall audio matching dog gait and speed.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>A-F002</td><td>System must play breathing audio that intensifies with exertion.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>A-F003</td><td>System must play obstacle-specific audio: bar rattle, contact zone impact, tunnel echo, weave pole brush.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>A-F004</td><td>System must play breed-specific vocalizations (barks, whines) based on dog state.</td><td>P2</td></tr><tr><td>A-F005</td><td>System must play fault audio: bar knock, wrong course buzzer, refusal whistle.</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.7.3.2 Handler Audio</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>A-F010</td><td>System must play handler footfall audio.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>A-F011</td><td>System must play handler command voice clips ("Come bye!", "Tunnel!", etc.).</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>A-F012</td><td>System must play handler exertion audio (sprint breathing).</td><td>P2</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.7.3.3 Crowd Audio</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>A-F020</td><td>System must play ambient crowd murmur during runs.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>A-F021</td><td>System must play reactive crowd audio: cheering for clean obstacles, gasps for near-misses, applause for completions.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>A-F022</td><td>System must scale crowd intensity based on run pressure (championship, personal best pace).</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>A-F023</td><td>System must provide crowd variation by venue (intimate indoor, large outdoor).</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.7.3.4 Environmental Audio</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>A-F030</td><td>System must play ambient environmental audio: wind, birds, PA system hum.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>A-F031</td><td>System must play venue-specific audio: indoor echo, outdoor openness.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>A-F032</td><td>System must play start countdown audio (beep... beep... beep GO).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>A-F033</td><td>System must play finish horn audio.</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.7.3.5 Music</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>A-F040</td><td>System must play subtle music bed during runs (low intensity, non-distracting).</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>A-F041</td><td>System must play victory theme on clean run completion.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>A-F042</td><td>System must play tension music during championship pressure moments.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>A-F043</td><td>Music must duck under commentary.</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.7.3.6 Dynamic Mix</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>A-F050</td><td>System must prioritize audio: commentary &gt; fault cues &gt; dog sounds &gt; crowd &gt; music.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>A-F051</td><td>System must support time-stretch for slow-motion replay (audio slows with video).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>A-F052</td><td>System must support configurable mix presets: default, commentary focus, immersive, minimal.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>A-F053</td><td>System must support individual volume controls: master, music, sfx, commentary, crowd.</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.7.4 Non-Functional Requirements</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>A-NF001</td><td>Audio system must use Wwise or Unity Audio with spatial audio support.</td></tr><tr><td>A-NF002</td><td>Audio latency must not exceed 50ms from event to sound.</td></tr><tr><td>A-NF003</td><td>Audio memory budget: ≤ 100MB for active audio assets.</td></tr><tr><td>A-NF004</td><td>Audio must support 5.1 surround and stereo configurations.</td></tr><tr><td>A-NF005</td><td>Audio must degrade gracefully on low-end hardware (reduce voices, disable reverb).</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.7.5 Dependencies</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Dependency</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Dog AI System</td><td>Dog audio triggers from dog state.</td></tr><tr><td>Scoring System</td><td>Fault audio triggers from fault events.</td></tr><tr><td>Commentary System</td><td>Commentary audio integration.</td></tr><tr><td>Replay System</td><td>Time-stretch support for slow-motion.</td></tr><tr><td>Crowd System</td><td>Crowd audio triggers from crowd state.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.7.6 Edge Cases / Failure States</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Edge Case</th><th>Resolution</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Audio device disconnected</td><td>Fallback to default device; display notification.</td></tr><tr><td>Audio memory exceeded</td><td>Unload least-recently-used assets; may cause brief silence.</td></tr><tr><td>Too many simultaneous sounds</td><td>Voice limiting; prioritize by mix hierarchy.</td></tr><tr><td>Spatial audio position invalid</td><td>Fallback to 2D panning.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.7.7 Acceptance Criteria</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Criterion</th><th>Verification</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>A-AC001</td><td>Dog footfalls sync with animation and speed.</td><td>Visual + audio inspection.</td></tr><tr><td>A-AC002</td><td>Obstacle audio plays correctly for each obstacle type.</td><td>Test each obstacle type.</td></tr><tr><td>A-AC003</td><td>Crowd audio reacts appropriately to run events.</td><td>Test clean run, fault run, near-miss.</td></tr><tr><td>A-AC004</td><td>Mix hierarchy correctly prioritizes commentary over other audio.</td><td>Test commentary during loud moments.</td></tr><tr><td>A-AC005</td><td>Time-stretch works correctly in slow-motion replay.</td><td>Test replay at various speeds.</td></tr><tr><td>A-AC006</td><td>All volume controls function correctly.</td><td>Adjust each control; verify effect.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h2>2.8 Commentary System</h2><h3>2.8.1 Overview</h3><p>The commentary system provides broadcast-style play-by-play and color commentary. It integrates with the Eastworld content generation pipeline for dialogue and ElevenLabs for voice synthesis. Commentary must feel responsive, contextual, and avoid repetition.</p><hr><h3>2.8.2 User Stories</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>User Story</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>CM-001</td><td>As a player, I want play-by-play commentary that describes what's happening in real-time.</td></tr><tr><td>CM-002</td><td>As a player, I want color commentary that adds insight and personality.</td></tr><tr><td>CM-003</td><td>As a player, I want breed callouts so the commentary feels specific to my dog.</td></tr><tr><td>CM-004</td><td>As a player, I want split-time callouts so I know if I'm on pace.</td></tr><tr><td>CM-005</td><td>As a player, I want fault reactions that acknowledge mistakes.</td></tr><tr><td>CM-006</td><td>As a player, I want championship pressure escalation in commentary.</td></tr><tr><td>CM-007</td><td>As a player, I want commentary to not repeat the same lines too frequently.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.8.3 Functional Requirements</h3><h4>2.8.3.1 Commentary Roles</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>CM-F001</td><td>System must support play-by-play commentator (primary, describes action).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>CM-F002</td><td>System must support color commentator (secondary, provides insight, reaction).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>CM-F003</td><td>System must support PA announcer (course announcements, results).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>CM-F004</td><td>Each role must have distinct voice and personality.</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.8.3.2 Trigger Events</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>CM-F010</td><td>Commentary must trigger on: run start, obstacle completion, split times, faults, near-misses, run completion, clean run, elimination, championship moments.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>CM-F011</td><td>Commentary must support breed callouts: mention breed name, breed characteristics, breed-specific strengths/challenges.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>CM-F012</td><td>Commentary must support handler callouts: handler name, handler backstory elements, handler statistics.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>CM-F013</td><td>Commentary must support venue callouts: venue name, venue characteristics.</td><td>P2</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.8.3.3 Line Selection</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>CM-F020</td><td>System must select appropriate line from available variants for each trigger event.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>CM-F021</td><td>System must use weighted random selection from eligible lines.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>CM-F022</td><td>System must track recently-played lines and avoid repetition within configurable window (default: 5 minutes).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>CM-F023</td><td>System must support line variants for different run contexts: leading, behind, close to SCT, championship pressure.</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.8.3.4 Timing and Cooldowns</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>CM-F030</td><td>Commentary must not interrupt itself; queue lines if both commentators have something to say.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>CM-F031</td><td>System must enforce cooldown between commentary lines (configurable, default: 3-8 seconds).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>CM-F032</td><td>System must prioritize commentary by importance: faults &gt; split times &gt; obstacle completion &gt; flavor.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>CM-F033</td><td>System must allow "urgent" lines to cut off less important lines.</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.8.3.5 Content Pipeline</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>CM-F040</td><td>Commentary lines must be generated via Eastworld pipeline or authored manually.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>CM-F041</td><td>Generated lines must be reviewed and approved before shipping.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>CM-F042</td><td>Lines must be converted to audio via ElevenLabs TTS or recorded voice.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>CM-F043</td><td>System must support line templates with variable interpolation: "[HandlerName] is really pushing the pace!", "[BreedName] showing excellent extension."</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>CM-F044</td><td>Commentary database must be extensible post-launch (DLC, updates).</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.8.4 Non-Functional Requirements</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>CM-NF001</td><td>Commentary line selection must complete within 10ms.</td></tr><tr><td>CM-NF002</td><td>Commentary audio files must be loaded on-demand or pre-cached per venue.</td></tr><tr><td>CM-NF003</td><td>Commentary must support localization (separate audio per language).</td></tr><tr><td>CM-NF004</td><td>Commentary must have memory budget: ≤ 50MB for active lines.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.8.5 Dependencies</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Dependency</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Eastworld Pipeline</td><td>Content generation for commentary lines.</td></tr><tr><td>ElevenLabs Pipeline</td><td>Voice synthesis for commentary audio.</td></tr><tr><td>Scoring System</td><td>Commentary triggers from scoring events.</td></tr><tr><td>Dog AI System</td><td>Commentary references dog state and breed.</td></tr><tr><td>Audio System</td><td>Commentary playback through audio system.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.8.6 Edge Cases / Failure States</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Edge Case</th><th>Resolution</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>No appropriate line available</td><td>Play generic line or silence.</td></tr><tr><td>Commentary falls behind live action</td><td>Skip queued lines; catch up to current moment.</td></tr><tr><td>Line template variable missing</td><td>Use fallback text (e.g., "The handler" if name missing).</td></tr><tr><td>Audio file fails to load</td><td>Skip line; continue commentary.</td></tr><tr><td>Player disables commentary</td><td>System stops commentary playback; other audio continues.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.8.7 Acceptance Criteria</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Criterion</th><th>Verification</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>CM-AC001</td><td>Commentary triggers appropriately for all defined events.</td><td>Test each trigger type.</td></tr><tr><td>CM-AC002</td><td>Commentary does not repeat lines within cooldown window.</td><td>Extended playtest; log line occurrences.</td></tr><tr><td>CM-AC003</td><td>Commentary reflects current run context (breed, handler, venue).</td><td>Test with multiple dog/handler/venue combinations.</td></tr><tr><td>CM-AC004</td><td>Commentary timing feels natural and not rushed.</td><td>Subjective playtest; user feedback.</td></tr><tr><td>CM-AC005</td><td>Generated lines are reviewed and approved.</td><td>Audit trail of content review.</td></tr><tr><td>CM-AC006</td><td>Commentary can be disabled via settings.</td><td>Settings test; verify silence.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h2>2.9 Crowd System</h2><h3>2.9.1 Overview</h3><p>The crowd system brings the arena to life with reactive spectators. Crowd behavior and audio respond to run performance, creating broadcast atmosphere and pressure feedback.</p><hr><h3>2.9.2 User Stories</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>User Story</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>CR-001</td><td>As a player, I want to see spectators in the arena so it feels like a real event.</td></tr><tr><td>CR-002</td><td>As a player, I want the crowd to react to my performance so my successes feel rewarding.</td></tr><tr><td>CR-003</td><td>As a player, I want crowd tension to build during close or high-stakes runs.</td></tr><tr><td>CR-004</td><td>As a player, I want broadcast-style crowd cutaways so the presentation feels professional.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.9.3 Functional Requirements</h3><h4>2.9.3.1 Crowd Rendering</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>CR-F001</td><td>System must render at least 200 spectators for premium presentation mode.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>CR-F002</td><td>System must support scalable crowd density: high (200+), medium (100), low (50), minimal (impostors only).</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>CR-F003</td><td>Crowd must be positioned around arena perimeter with realistic seating/standing distribution.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>CR-F004</td><td>Individual spectators must have varied appearance: clothing, accessories, poses.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>CR-F005</td><td>Crowd must use LOD system: 3D models close to camera, impostors/card sprites for distant spectators.</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.9.3.2 Crowd Reactions</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>CR-F010</td><td>Crowd must react to: run start, obstacle completion, near-misses, faults, clean run completion, elimination, personal best pace.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>CR-F011</td><td>Reaction intensity must scale with run importance (training &lt; exhibition &lt; career &lt; championship).</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>CR-F012</td><td>Reactions must include: standing/sitting transitions, arm movements, head turning toward action.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>CR-F013</td><td>Reactions must be distributed spatially (nearby spectators react first, reaction spreads).</td><td>P2</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.9.3.3 Broadcast Integration</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>CR-F020</td><td>System must support broadcast camera cutaways to crowd reaction shots.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>CR-F021</td><td>System must identify "hero spectators" for close-up shots during reactions.</td><td>P2</td></tr><tr><td>CR-F022</td><td>Crowd cutaways must be triggered at appropriate moments (post-fault, post-run, during pauses).</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.9.4 Non-Functional Requirements</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>CR-NF001</td><td>Crowd rendering must not exceed 3ms frame time budget.</td></tr><tr><td>CR-NF002</td><td>Crowd animations must use GPU instancing or equivalent optimization.</td></tr><tr><td>CR-NF003</td><td>Crowd must not consume more than 50MB texture memory.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.9.5 Dependencies</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Dependency</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Scoring System</td><td>Crowd reacts to scoring events.</td></tr><tr><td>Camera System</td><td>Broadcast cutaways to crowd.</td></tr><tr><td>Audio System</td><td>Crowd audio synchronized with visual reactions.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.9.6 Edge Cases / Failure States</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Edge Case</th><th>Resolution</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Performance budget exceeded</td><td>Reduce crowd density dynamically.</td></tr><tr><td>Spectator position invalid</td><td>Reposition to valid location.</td></tr><tr><td>Reaction triggered too frequently</td><td>Clamp reaction frequency; idle state minimum.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.9.7 Acceptance Criteria</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Criterion</th><th>Verification</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>CR-AC001</td><td>Crowd density meets target on recommended hardware.</td><td>Frame time test with 200 spectators.</td></tr><tr><td>CR-AC002</td><td>Crowd reacts appropriately to run events.</td><td>Test each trigger event; verify reaction.</td></tr><tr><td>CR-AC003</td><td>Crowd scales down appropriately on low-end hardware.</td><td>Test on minimum spec; verify reduced density.</td></tr><tr><td>CR-AC004</td><td>Crowd cutaways integrate smoothly with broadcast presentation.</td><td>Visual inspection of replay/cutaway integration.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h2>2.10 Progression / Career / Unlock System</h2><h3>2.10.1 Overview</h3><p>The progression system provides long-term motivation through career mode, unlockable content, and persistent records. Players earn access to new handlers, dogs, and venues through gameplay achievements.</p><hr><h3>2.10.2 User Stories</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>User Story</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>PR-001</td><td>As a player, I want a career mode with progression so I have long-term goals.</td></tr><tr><td>PR-002</td><td>As a player, I want to unlock new dogs and handlers so I can expand my options.</td></tr><tr><td>PR-003</td><td>As a player, I want to track my performance records so I can see improvement.</td></tr><tr><td>PR-004</td><td>As a player, I want increasing difficulty as I progress so the challenge scales.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.10.3 Functional Requirements</h3><h4>2.10.3.1 Career Structure</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>PR-F001</td><td>Career must include multiple events/tournaments with increasing difficulty.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>PR-F002</td><td>Career must have unlock progression: complete earlier events to unlock later ones.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>PR-F003</td><td>Career must track: runs completed, clean runs, faults accumulated, placements, titles earned.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>PR-F004</td><td>Career must support multiple difficulty tiers: Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced, Masters.</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.10.3.2 Unlockables</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>PR-F010</td><td>System must support unlockable dogs with different breeds and stats.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>PR-F011</td><td>System must support unlockable handlers with different stats and appearances.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>PR-F012</td><td>System must support unlockable venues.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>PR-F013</td><td>Unlock conditions must include: career progress, achievement completion, cumulative statistics.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>PR-F014</td><td>Unlocks must be clearly communicated to player when earned.</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.10.3.3 Persistent Records</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>PR-F020</td><td>System must track personal best times per course per difficulty.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>PR-F021</td><td>System must track career statistics: total runs, clean run percentage, average faults, fastest times.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>PR-F022</td><td>Records must persist across sessions via save system.</td><td>P0</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.10.4 Non-Functional Requirements</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>PR-NF001</td><td>Progression data must be serializable and version-tolerant for updates.</td></tr><tr><td>PR-NF002</td><td>Unlock conditions must be data-driven (ScriptableObjects) for designer iteration.</td></tr><tr><td>PR-NF003</td><td>Progression calculations must not impact gameplay performance.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.10.5 Dependencies</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Dependency</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Save System</td><td>Progression requires persistent storage.</td></tr><tr><td>Scoring System</td><td>Unlocks triggered by performance.</td></tr><tr><td>Dog/Handler Data</td><td>Unlockable content requires asset definitions.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.10.6 Edge Cases / Failure States</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Edge Case</th><th>Resolution</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Save corruption</td><td>Rebuild progression from cloud backup or start fresh with notification.</td></tr><tr><td>Unlock condition invalid</td><td>Fallback to default unlock path; log error.</td></tr><tr><td>Cheat/hack detection (if applicable)</td><td>Leaderboard invalidation; local progression unaffected.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.10.7 Acceptance Criteria</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Criterion</th><th>Verification</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>PR-AC001</td><td>Career progression works correctly across multiple sessions.</td><td>Save/load test with progression.</td></tr><tr><td>PR-AC002</td><td>Unlocks trigger correctly when conditions are met.</td><td>Test each unlock condition.</td></tr><tr><td>PR-AC003</td><td>Records persist and display correctly.</td><td>Verify record accuracy after multiple runs.</td></tr><tr><td>PR-AC004</td><td>Career difficulty scales appropriately.</td><td>Playtest progression from beginner to masters.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h2>2.11 Online Features System</h2><h3>2.11.1 Overview</h3><p>Online features include leaderboards, ghost runs, and cloud saves. Multiplayer is explicitly excluded from v1 scope but architecture should not preclude future addition.</p><hr><h3>2.11.2 User Stories</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>User Story</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>ON-001</td><td>As a player, I want to see how my times compare to other players on leaderboards.</td></tr><tr><td>ON-002</td><td>As a player, I want to race against ghost runs from other players.</td></tr><tr><td>ON-003</td><td>As a player, I want my progression saved to the cloud so I can play on multiple devices.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.11.3 Functional Requirements</h3><h4>2.11.3.1 Leaderboards</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>ON-F001</td><td>System must display global leaderboards per course per difficulty.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>ON-F002</td><td>System must display friends-only leaderboard view (if platform friends available).</td><td>P2</td></tr><tr><td>ON-F003</td><td>Leaderboards must show: rank, player name, time, faults, date.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>ON-F004</td><td>Leaderboards must support filtering by: dog breed, handler, date range.</td><td>P2</td></tr><tr><td>ON-F005</td><td>Leaderboards must update within 30 seconds of run completion.</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.11.3.2 Ghost Runs</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>ON-F010</td><td>System must allow players to race against ghost runs (visual replay of other players' runs).</td><td>P2</td></tr><tr><td>ON-F011</td><td>Ghost runs must be downloadable from leaderboard entries.</td><td>P2</td></tr><tr><td>ON-F012</td><td>Ghost runs must display ghost name and time during race.</td><td>P2</td></tr><tr><td>ON-F013</td><td>Ghost runs must be recordable and uploadable by players.</td><td>P2</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>2.11.3.3 Cloud Saves</h4><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>ON-F020</td><td>System must sync progression and settings to cloud storage.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>ON-F021</td><td>System must resolve save conflicts (latest timestamp wins, with user prompt for manual resolution option).</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>ON-F022</td><td>System must support offline play with sync when connection restored.</td><td>P1</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.11.4 Non-Functional Requirements</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>ON-NF001</td><td>Online features must use platform-agnostic backend (Steam, GOG, future console).</td></tr><tr><td>ON-NF002</td><td>Leaderboard queries must complete within 3 seconds.</td></tr><tr><td>ON-NF003</td><td>Ghost run data must not exceed 100KB per run.</td></tr><tr><td>ON-NF004</td><td>Online features must gracefully degrade when offline.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.11.5 Dependencies</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Dependency</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Save System</td><td>Cloud save integration.</td></tr><tr><td>Replay System</td><td>Ghost run data format.</td></tr><tr><td>Platform Services</td><td>Steam/GOG/console SDK integration.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.11.6 Edge Cases / Failure States</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Edge Case</th><th>Resolution</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Network unavailable</td><td>Disable online features; show offline indicator.</td></tr><tr><td>Leaderboard query timeout</td><td>Show cached data or "unavailable" message.</td></tr><tr><td>Cloud save conflict</td><td>Prompt user to choose or auto-resolve with latest.</td></tr><tr><td>Ghost run desync</td><td>Abort ghost display; show "ghost unavailable" message.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.11.7 Acceptance Criteria</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Criterion</th><th>Verification</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>ON-AC001</td><td>Leaderboards display correct rankings.</td><td>Test with multiple submissions; verify ordering.</td></tr><tr><td>ON-AC002</td><td>Ghost runs play accurately against live run.</td><td>Race against ghost; verify synchronization.</td></tr><tr><td>ON-AC003</td><td>Cloud saves sync correctly across devices.</td><td>Test on two devices with same account.</td></tr><tr><td>ON-AC004</td><td>Offline mode works correctly.</td><td>Disconnect network; verify gameplay continues.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h2>2.12 Accessibility System</h2><h3>2.12.1 Overview</h3><p>Accessibility features ensure the game is playable by users with various disabilities. Features include visual, auditory, and motor accessibility options.</p><hr><h3>2.12.2 User Stories</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>User Story</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>AC-001</td><td>As a colorblind player, I want colorblind modes so I can distinguish game elements.</td></tr><tr><td>AC-002</td><td>As a player with low vision, I want scalable UI and high contrast modes.</td></tr><tr><td>AC-003</td><td>As a deaf or hard-of-hearing player, I want visual cues for audio events.</td></tr><tr><td>AC-004</td><td>As a player with motor impairments, I want configurable input timing and sensitivity.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.12.3 Functional Requirements</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>AC-F001</td><td>System must provide colorblind modes: protanopia, deuteranopia, tritanopia, achromatopsia.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>AC-F002</td><td>System must provide high contrast mode for UI and gameplay elements.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>AC-F003</td><td>System must provide UI scaling from 75% to 150%.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>AC-F004</td><td>System must provide visual indicators for all audio cues: fault sounds, countdown, command acknowledgment.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>AC-F005</td><td>System must provide configurable input hold times for timed interactions.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>AC-F006</td><td>System must provide configurable input sensitivity curves.</td><td>P1</td></tr><tr><td>AC-F007</td><td>System must support toggle vs. hold for sprint and other sustained inputs.</td><td>P0</td></tr><tr><td>AC-F008</td><td>System must provide one-handed play mode (remap all functions to one side of controller/keyboard).</td><td>P2</td></tr><tr><td>AC-F009</td><td>System must provide auto-camera option for players who cannot manually control camera.</td><td>P2</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.12.4 Non-Functional Requirements</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>AC-NF001</td><td>All accessibility settings must be accessible from main menu without entering gameplay.</td></tr><tr><td>AC-NF002</td><td>Accessibility settings must persist across sessions.</td></tr><tr><td>AC-NF003</td><td>Accessibility features must be compatible with platform accessibility APIs where available.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.12.5 Acceptance Criteria</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Criterion</th><th>Verification</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>AC-AC001</td><td>All colorblind modes correctly adjust color-dependent elements.</td><td>Colorblind simulation test.</td></tr><tr><td>AC-AC002</td><td>UI scaling works correctly at all supported scales.</td><td>Test at 75%, 100%, 125%, 150%.</td></tr><tr><td>AC-AC003</td><td>Visual cues fire for all audio events.</td><td>Disable audio; verify visual indicators.</td></tr><tr><td>AC-AC004</td><td>Toggle/hold settings work correctly for all applicable inputs.</td><td>Test each toggleable input.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h2>2.13 Performance / Optimization / Platform Constraints</h2><h3>2.13.1 Overview</h3><p>Performance requirements ensure the game runs smoothly across target platforms with appropriate scaling for different hardware capabilities.</p><hr><h3>2.13.2 User Stories</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>User Story</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>PF-001</td><td>As a player, I want smooth frame rate on my hardware.</td></tr><tr><td>PF-002</td><td>As a player on lower-end hardware, I want the game to scale appropriately.</td></tr><tr><td>PF-003</td><td>As a player, I want fast loading times.</td></tr></tbody></table><hr><h3>2.13.3 Functional Requirements</h3><table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Requirement</th><th>Priority</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>PF-F001</td><td>Game must maintain 60fps on recommended spec
